data.title: As a non-engineer user, the installer writes the machine-local dso.plugin_root to dso-config.conf so all DSO skills work on first launch
data.description:
New story identified during preplanning adversarial review (Phase 2.5). Critical gap: all /dso:skill invocations fail if dso.plugin_root is not set to the installed plugin path on the target machine.

Done Definition:
- Installer determines the absolute path to the DSO plugin after clone: either (a) ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/digital-service-orchestra/ if DSO is installed via Claude Code marketplace, or (b) a local dev path; the installer detects which scenario applies
- dso.plugin_root=<absolute_path> written to .claude/dso-config.conf in the scaffolded project directory after git clone completes (appended or replaced via sed; the placeholder in the template's dso-config.conf is replaced, not duplicated)
- The DSO shim (.claude/scripts/dso in the scaffolded project) correctly resolves dso.plugin_root at invocation time from dso-config.conf
- After this step runs, executing .claude/scripts/dso ticket list inside the scaffolded project exits 0 (functional smoke test embedded in installer)
- All T1 tests covering S7 behavior pass (GREEN)

Considerations:
- dso.plugin_root is a machine-local absolute path — it CANNOT be baked into the template fork (different machines have different home directories). Must be written by the installer at install time on the user's machine.
- If the DSO plugin install step (S5) runs after S7 (e.g., plugin install is the final step), S7 may need to be deferred until after S5, or the installer must detect the plugin path dynamically after installation. Evaluate the ordering with S5 and ensure the smoke test (dso ticket list) runs after plugin install.

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
